source_code:
# Reproducer derived from https://github.com/Nike-Inc/gimme-aws-creds/issues/302
# repo: Nike-Inc/gimme-aws-creds
# title: Auth Failures to OKTA - auth_result redirect_uri failures due to localhost

# Last frames in traceback:
#   /Users/$SCRUBBED/.asdf/installs/python/3.9.5/lib/python3.9/site-packages/gimme_aws_creds/main.py line 647 in aws_app

#   /Users/$SCRUBBED/.asdf/installs/python/3.9.5/lib/python3.9/site-packages/gimme_aws_creds/main.py line 582 in auth_session
#   /Users/$SCRUBBED/.asdf/installs/python/3.9.5/lib/python3.9/site-packages/gimme_aws_creds/okta.py line 183 in auth_session
#   /Users/$SCRUBBED/.asdf/installs/python/3.9.5/lib/python3.9/site-packages/requests/cookies.py line 328 in __getitem__
#   /Users/$SCRUBBED/.asdf/installs/python/3.9.5/lib/python3.9/site-packages/requests/cookies.py line 399 in _find_no_duplicates

# Minimal repro (conceptual):

def repro():
    # Conditions described in the issue:
    # <!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
    # ## Expected Behavior
    # I should be able to auth against OKTA after entering my password and MFA token
    # ## Current Behavior
    # Starting this afternoon ( with no updates to the gimme-aws-creds application) numerous people encountered the exception 
    raise KeyError('reproduce: see https://github.com/Nike-Inc/gimme-aws-creds/issues/302')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    repro()
expected_fix_hint: solution got the application working again.  In short it looks like http://localhost:8080/login was previously accepted but now it's not and we get a 403 response back.
